title: Vault addr= param could enable SSRF if URI comes from partially-trusted input
description: VaultBackend::from_uri accepts addr=<url> as a query parameter, overriding VAULT_ADDR. If a URI is constructed from partially-trusted input (e.g. user-supplied config, database rows), an attacker can redirect vault requests to secretx://vault/secret/foo?addr=http://attacker.com. This is SSRF. The library design assumes URIs come from trusted admin config — that assumption is not documented. Maintenance consequence: users who add URI-from-user-input flows will introduce SSRF silently. Fix: document the trust assumption on from_uri, and/or reject non-localhost addrs without an explicit opt-in.
